questions.specification: When designing a manual pedal-powered irrigation pump, which physiological factor is primarily monitored to assess the aerobic energy expenditure of the user during operation?

Professional landscape gardeners often use cordless electric hedge trimmers for extended periods. These tools must be designed to accommodate the physiological limits of the human body to ensure both efficiency and user well-being.

**Figure 1: A professional cordless hedge trimmer**

![A professional grade cordless hedge trimmer with an ergonomic wrap-around handle and battery pack at the rear.](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/61be7c9b-6490-449e-8742-50d4847e0c4f)

parts.content: Identify **two** factors that influence an individual's physical work capacity when operating heavy outdoor power tools.
parts.markscheme:
- Muscle strength (determined by age or gender). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Cardiovascular fitness/endurance. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- General health/nutrition levels. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain why the **grip diameter** of the trimmer's handle is a critical physiological factor in the tool's design.
parts.markscheme:
- An appropriate grip diameter allows for a 'power grip' where the fingers and thumb overlap. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This maximises the surface area of contact to distribute the force/pressure across the palm. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Reducing localized muscle strain and preventing premature hand fatigue. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain **two** ways that environmental stressors can impact the physiological performance of the operator.
parts.markscheme:
- **Thermal stress/Ambient temperature:** <marks>1 mark</marks>
- High temperatures cause the body to divert blood to the skin for cooling (sweating), which increases heart rate. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This leads to faster exhaustion/dehydration and a decline in physical coordination. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Noise levels:** <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Constant exposure to high-decibel noise increases physiological stress levels (cortisol). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This can lead to increased blood pressure and reduced concentration/mental fatigue. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how the management of **Hand-Arm Vibration Syndrome (HAVS)** in the design of the tool can improve the safety, fatigue levels, and long-term health of the operator.
parts.markscheme:
**Safety:**
- Vibration-damping materials (like elastomers) are used in the handles. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This prevents the 'white finger' effect where blood flow is restricted. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Ensuring the user maintains full tactile sensitivity to keep a secure grip and avoid accidental drops. <marks>1 mark</marks>

**Fatigue:**
- Reducing high-frequency oscillations reduces the workload on the small muscles of the hand and forearm. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This prevents the muscles from having to constantly contract to stabilize the tool. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Allowing the gardener to operate the tool for longer durations without significant loss of motor control. <marks>1 mark</marks>

**Long-term Health:**
- Ergonomic design isolates the motor from the chassis using internal damping mounts. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This limits the cumulative exposure of joints and nerves to mechanical shock. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Preventing permanent debilitating conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or permanent nerve damage. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which of the following physiological factors is most likely to affect the design of a hand tool for prolonged use?

An ergonomics researcher measured the average time taken for a participant to react to various sensory inputs during a simulated industrial task. The results are recorded in the table below.

| Sensory Input | Mean Reaction Time (ms) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Visual (Flashing Signal) | 245 |
| Haptic (Vibration) | 205 |
| Auditory (Warning Tone) | 165 |
| Visual (Text Display) | 390 |

Based on this physiological data, which sensory input should be prioritized for a time-critical safety alert?
When designing a manual water pump for use in remote areas, which physiological factor is the primary determinant of how long a user can sustain the required pumping action?
Which physiological change occurs most prominently when a worker maintains a sustained static posture during a manual task?

options.content: Accelerated loss of mineral density in the skeletal structure
A significant decline in the rate of carbon dioxide production
Immediate cooling of the localized muscle tissue due to inactivity
Restricted blood flow caused by sustained internal muscle pressure
